Authority To Use Appropriations To Enhance Claims Processing Capacity and Automation, , , provided that:Pub. L. 117–168, title VII, § 701(a)136 Stat. 1794Aug. 10, 2022“The Secretary of Veterans Affairs may use, from amounts appropriated to the Cost of War Toxic Exposures Fund established by , United States Code, as added by section 805 of this Act, such amounts as may be necessary to continue the modernization, development, and expansion of capabilities and capacity of information technology systems and infrastructure of the Veterans Benefits Administration, including for claims automation, to support expected increased claims processing for newly eligible veterans pursuant to this Act [see Short Title of 2022 Amendment note set out under ].”section 324 of title 38section 101 of this title

children.heading: .—Establishment
children.label: a .—Establishment
children.text: There is hereby established in the Treasury of the United States an account to be known as the “Cost of War Toxic Exposures Fund” (the “Fund”), to be administered by the Secretary.

children.heading: .—Deposits
children.label: b .—Deposits
children.text: There shall be deposited in the Fund such amounts as may be appropriated to the Fund pursuant to subsection (c).

the delivery of veterans’ health care associated with exposure to environmental hazards in the active military, naval, air, or space service in programs administered by the Under Secretary for Health;

any expenses incident to the delivery of veterans’ health care and benefits associated with exposure to environmental hazards in the active military, naval, air, or space service, including administrative expenses, such as information technology and claims processing and appeals, and excluding leases as authorized or approved under ; andsection 8104 of this title
medical and other research relating to exposure to environmental hazards.

children.label: A
children.text: in the baseline for purposes of section 257 of the Balanced Budget and Emergency Deficit Control Act of 1985 ();2 U.S.C. 907

children.label: B
children.text: by the Chairman of the Committee on the Budget of the Senate and the Chair of the Committee on the Budget of the House of Representatives, as appropriate, for purposes of budget enforcement in the Senate and the House of Representatives;

children.label: C
children.text: under the Congressional Budget Act of 1974 ( et seq.), including in the reports required by section 308(b) of such Act (); and2 U.S.C. 6212 U.S.C. 6391So in original. Probably should be “639(b));”.1

children.label: D
children.text: for purposes of the Statutory Pay-As-You-Go Act of 2010 ( et seq.).2 U.S.C. 931

No amount appropriated to the Fund in fiscal year 2023 or any subsequent fiscal year pursuant to this section shall be counted as discretionary budget authority and outlays or as direct spending for any estimate of an appropriation Act under the Congressional Budget and Impoundment Control Act of 1974 ( et seq.) and any other Act.2 U.S.C. 621

Notwithstanding the Budget Scorekeeping Guidelines and the accompanying list of programs and accounts set forth in the joint explanatory statement of the committee of conference accompanying Conference Report 105–217, and for purposes of the Balanced Budget and Emergency Deficit Control Act of 1985 ( et seq.) and the Congressional Budget Act of 1974 ( et seq.), the Fund shall be treated as if it were an account designated as “Appropriated Entitlements and Mandatories for Fiscal Year 1997” in the joint explanatory statement of the committee of conference accompanying Conference Report 105–217.2 U.S.C. 9002 U.S.C. 621

children.heading: .—Estimates for Congressional Consideration
children.label: e .—Estimates for Congressional Consideration
children.text: The Secretary shall include in documents submitted to Congress in support of the President’s budget submitted pursuant to detailed estimates of the sums described in subsection (c) for the applicable fiscal year.section 1105 of title 31

children.heading: .—Procedures for Estimates
children.label: f .—Procedures for Estimates
children.text: The Secretary may, after consultation with the Committee on Appropriations of the Senate and the Committee on Appropriations of the House of Representatives, establish policies and procedures for developing the annual detailed estimates required by subsection (e).
